summary:
Sodium benzoate and phenylacetate are primary drugs used to reduce ammonia in urea cycle disorders by acting as nitrogen scavengers. Although phenylbutyrate is commonly associated with this role due to its structural similarity and shared metabolism pathway, direct evidence highlights phenylacetate as the more specifically cited agent among established therapies.  

\boxed{B}
